signals.headline: Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.7% YoY | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's annual inflation rate rose to 2.7% in March 2026 from 1.9% in February, confirming preliminary estimates and marking the highest since January 2024, driven by a 7.2% energy price surge including 20% fuel and 44.4% heating oil increases.[1]  
This acceleration exceeds prior trends and could shift ECB rate cut e
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% YoY | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in prior months, below market forecasts of 2.4% but amid Middle East energy shocks.[2]  
Bank of Korea recently held rates at 2.5% and raised its 2026 inflation outlook to 2.2% from 2.1%, signaling contained pressures.[2]

signals.headline: **EC notice to LDF convener (April 3):** Election Commission issued notice to TP Ramakrishnan (LDF, contesting Perambra
signals.raw: **EC notice to LDF convener (April 3):** Election Commission issued notice to TP Ramakrishnan (LDF, contesting Perambra assembly seat) over a campaign vehicle announcement accusing IUML of communal campaigning; LDF denies authorization, UDF complains—unlikely to shift markets as it's investigative, not resolved.[1]

signals.raw: **IUML dominance claims in UDF (April 11):** Youth League leader asserts IUML deserves DyCM post if UDF wins, projecting 22+ seats; PK Kunhalikutty downplays, but rank-and-file pushes for power share—speculative pre-election posturing, low market impact.[4]

signals.headline: **Other context:** Kerala Lok Sabha voting set for April 26 (not assembly); IUML strongholds noted in Ponnani/Malappuram
signals.raw: **Other context:** Kerala Lok Sabha voting set for April 26 (not assembly); IUML strongholds noted in Ponnani/Malappuram; Samastha critiques IUML women candidates (undated Instagram)—no ties to "Vance" or fresh resolutions.[2][3]
